description:
Migrate Python from single-root pyproject.toml to a uv workspace, parallel to the Cargo workspace pattern.

Goals:
- Each Python package in src/python/{cargo,buck,buildsystem,cfg} becomes a real uv workspace member with its own pyproject.toml.
- Examples src/examples/python-hello and python-hello-deps also become members so users can demo the parallel uv track.
- Imports move from python.<name> to turnkey.<name> (PEP 420 namespace package).
- uv sync produces a working venv with all members editable + externals resolved.
- Buck2 build path unchanged: pydeps cell still built from pylock.toml via pydeps-gen.
- Downstream monorepos can adopt the same pattern with their own namespace (acme.<name>, etc.).

Decisions locked in (conversation 2026-05-15):
- Namespace: turnkey.<name> (PEP 420 namespace package; no turnkey/__init__.py anywhere).
- Build backend: hatchling, with [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel.force-include] '.' = 'turnkey/<name>' to remap source without physically restructuring src/python/*.
- Root pyproject.toml: stays a [project] with dev optional-deps; gains [tool.uv.workspace].
- Examples are workspace members (not excluded).

Implementation risk to verify when starting child 1: hatchling force-include behavior in editable installs (uv installs members editable). Fallback is hatch sources+packages or an editables shim — solvable, just may not be one line.
title: Adopt uv workspaces for Python with turnkey.* namespace
